approach: one spec at a time; the retired corpus is reference only, nothing is migrated or copied
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the current implementation, never evidence of current behavior
additive scenarios only on the frozen suite; any narrowing would stop the mission
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: low — one node's suite plus one stating line in the skill it specs; no engine, no shared instrument, no other suite touched
reversibility: high — additive scenarios on an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any pre-existing contract
novelty: low — the node, its squad and its suite all already exist; this widens a contract rather than introducing a capability
confidence: high — four cold judge rounds, final verdict aligned with no open markers

why: Built the live suite up using the retired corpus as reference only — nothing migrated. Of fourteen candidate legacy cases, seven were rejected before authoring: three STALE (two assert a description prefix a later change request reversed corpus-wide, one requires naming a skill since deleted whose successor the live suite already names — each would now penalize a correct agent), four UNSUPPORTED (the implementation has no such rule, so a scenario would spec aspiration as behavior). Seven scenarios added, structurally additive by gherkin-cli (added seven, zero modified, zero removed) so the freeze is preserved and no re-open was entered. Four cold spec-judge rounds: round one dropped a scenario whose derivation had a branch point the implementation never took, round two split a double-barreled scenario and corrected an over-claim, round three added an atomicity companion after a naive string heuristic was shown to pass the original alone and relocated an assertion that a correct check-then-fix implementation would have failed, round four corrected two spec defects — prose that contradicted its own frozen scenario, and transient change-request narration that had severed the use-case table. Final verdict ALIGNED with no open markers and no blocking defect. Root project status and its human ratification left untouched: additive scenarios self-clear as a detail-adjustment.

Cold impl-judge re-derived every frozen scenario's oracle independently and exercised the suite with blind probes: all pass. The added scenarios exposed three real implementation gaps, each closed in deliver — a drafting gate the skill never stated, an over-split the atomicity rule left unbarred, and a report bullet a rollup could satisfy while hiding failures below the fix bar. No pre-existing scenario regressed; the improve-path blast risk was probed directly and held. One scenario failed the first gate: a routing overlap where this node claimed the improve case in prose but offered the harness only create-shaped phrasing, so a sibling that enumerates its targets captured it. Repaired by naming the object this node owns rather than the verb the sibling owns, in a description shorter than the one it replaced; blind routing probes then cleared every routing scenario, including requests placed deliberately in the overlap, which correctly still yield to the sibling when the framing is diagnostic. The node spec was rebuilt to the current four-section shape with its control flow drawn as a graph and every edge bound one-to-one to its covering scenario, checked mechanically in both directions — that drawing is what surfaced the routing defect.

note: The eval loop declares which measurement layers run, but a declared layer that matches no scenario is a silent no-op — nothing warns. The reverse also holds: suites carry layer-tagged scenarios with no policy file at all, and one layer is tagged in suites yet declared nowhere, so those scenarios are skipped and never reported. Corpus-wide, not specific to this node.

A routing scenario can pass a gate by inspection of the skill descriptions rather than by blind activation runs. This mission's first gate did exactly that and returned a false pass on a scenario later shown to fail. The judge contract should require routing and activation scenarios be graded by blind runs.

The rule-atomicity scenarios leave one cheap proxy alive: a test keyed on whether the second clause carries a back-reference decides every current fixture correctly. Closing it needs a qualifying clause that carries no back-reference and must still be kept whole.
The quality fix bar's lower boundary is unassertable from the suite. That a failure below the bar is left unfixed exists only as a presupposition inside one scenario's setup; no scenario decides it.

Content mixing enforceable criteria with action steps has no stated disposition — the implementation supports authoring criteria only, redirecting the whole document, or splitting it in two, and picks none. A scenario was drafted and dropped this mission because freezing one branch would have been an arbitrary choice. The implementation must state the behavior before a scenario can bind it.
